title: Don’t Follow Your Passion | Ben Horowitz’s Advice for New Graduates
summary: In this commencement address to Columbia University's Fu Foundation School of Engineering and Applied Science Class of 2015, Ben Horowitz challenges some of the most common advice given to graduates. Rather than urging students to “follow their passion,” Horowitz argues that people should focus on developing their strengths and making meaningful contributions to the world. Drawing on stories from his own time at Columbia, the founding of technology companies, and investments in startups like Airbnb, he explores the importance of independent thinking, conviction, and pursuing ideas that othe...
score_reason: The topic offers a valuable perspective on career advice relevant to professionals, but it is somewhat generic and requires a personal angle to maximize impact.
partner_takeaway: Focusing on developing strengths and making meaningful contributions is a more effective career strategy than simply following passion.
partner_summary: Ben Horowitz delivers a commencement address challenging the common advice to 'follow your passion,' advocating instead for focusing on strengths and meaningful work. This matters to professionals who often hear generic career advice but need actionable perspectives on career growth. Readers should reconsider how they approach career decisions, emphasizing skill development and impact over passion alone.

title: 🧠 Community Wisdom: Quarterly planning and AI, cash vs. equity comp, paying for interview exercises, AI-powered outbound, compliance startup opportunities, and more
score_reason: The item covers multiple practical topics relevant to business and product leaders, offering tactical tips and trend insights from a community perspective.
partner_takeaway: Community-driven insights provide practical approaches to quarterly planning, compensation strategies, AI-powered sales, and compliance startup opportunities.
partner_summary: This edition of Community Wisdom shares diverse insights on quarterly planning, cash versus equity compensation, paying for interview exercises, AI-powered outbound sales, and compliance startup opportunities. These topics are directly relevant to business leaders and product managers looking for actionable strategies and market trends. The audience should explore these community-sourced tactics and trends to refine their operational and strategic approaches.
